name: Intentional Sophism
strauss_status: EXPLICITLY NAMED — REQUIRES FURTHER DOCUMENTARY DEVELOPMENT

source.primary_work: Persecution and the Art of Writing
source.primary_essay: The Literary Character of the Guide for the Perplexed
source.source_formulation: Strauss refers to "intentional sophisms" as devices used by Maimonides, though he does not elaborate them in this immediate passage.
source.documentary_limit: The present passage names the device but does not classify its subtypes.

mechanism: A bounded argument appears to support a conclusion but contains a documented logical, semantic, evidentiary, or inferential defect that materially shapes the reader's apparent conclusion. The current source passage does not authorize a subtype taxonomy or permit intention to be inferred from the defect alone.
investigative_requirement: Reconstruct the argument exactly; distinguish premises, inference steps, and conclusion; identify the alleged defect under a stated logical or evidentiary standard; test whether the defect materially affects the conclusion; investigate ordinary error, compression, pedagogy, translation, corruption, and speaker alternatives; and keep sophistic structure separate from intentional deployment.
documentary_constraints: No canonical subtype classification is authorized by the present source. Any descriptive defect label remains local and noncanonical.
A false premise, invalid inference, misleading formulation, or omitted qualification may establish a candidate sophistic structure but not intention.
The word intentional belongs to Strauss's name for the device; the evaluator must not convert that name into proof of intention in a newly examined passage.
A result may become a corroborated sophistic structure after adversarial testing, while intentionality remains explicitly unresolved.
Direct documentary evidence of intention may be attached to an inquiry record, but this v0.1 evaluator does not adjudicate authorial intention.
LC-010 must not be collapsed into contradiction, ambiguity, irony, omission, or misquotation; those devices require separate evaluations.

trigger_conditions: A bounded argument is recoverable in a stable witness.
Premises, inference steps, and conclusion can be reconstructed without silently repairing the argument.
A specific inferential, logical, semantic, or evidentiary defect is documented under an explicit standard.
The alleged defect materially affects the apparent support for the conclusion.
The defect is not merely disagreement with the conclusion.
minimum_evidence: Exact argument text and stable witness location.
Declared textual boundary of the argument.
Premise records preserving explicit and reconstructed premises separately.
A precise description of the alleged defect.
The logical, semantic, or evidentiary standard used to identify the defect.
A counterfactual reconstruction showing whether correction of the defect changes the conclusion or its support.
Resolved speaker, quotation, objection, hypothetical, and reported-opinion attribution.
Source-language, translation, edition, punctuation, and textual-variant review.
An auditable evidence path from witness text through argument reconstruction to the defect claim.
corroboration_indicators: The same defect pattern recurs under comparable conditions elsewhere in the work.
The author elsewhere supplies the missing distinction or correction.
A source comparison shows a controlled alteration that creates the defective inference.
Another independently reconstructed literary device converges on the same argument.
The author demonstrates competence with the governing distinction elsewhere.
The defective argument produces a predictable surface conclusion contradicted by stronger documentary evidence elsewhere.
ordinary_alternatives: Compressed argument with an unstated but recoverable premise.
Textual corruption, punctuation, or witness variation.
Different speaker, objection, quotation, or hypothetical.
Change of domain, scope, modality, or definition.
The investigator has reconstructed the argument incorrectly.
The conclusion remains independently supported despite the alleged defect.
disqualifying_conditions: No bounded argument can be reconstructed.
The alleged defect is only disagreement with the conclusion.
The defect disappears when explicit premises or context are restored.
Correcting the alleged defect does not materially change the argument's support.
The passage belongs to another speaker, quotation, objection, or hypothetical not yet resolved.
Translation, textual corruption, punctuation, or witness variation fully explains the problem.
The analysis silently introduces or removes a premise.
The defect depends on an anachronistic logical or semantic standard.
The argument is merely incomplete rather than misleading or fallacious.
Source integrity or argument attribution remains unresolved.
authorized_response: Preserve the complete argument and witness.
Create separate premise, inference-step, and conclusion records.
Mark explicit and reconstructed premises distinctly.
Document the alleged defect and governing standard.
Generate a counterfactual corrected argument without overwriting the original.
Test all ordinary error, compression, pedagogical, rhetorical, textual, and attribution alternatives.
Search the work for corrections, repetitions, parallel arguments, and counterexamples.
Evaluate LC-003, LC-004, LC-005, LC-006, LC-011, LC-014, or LC-015 separately when independently triggered.
Preserve intentionality as unresolved unless direct documentary evidence is separately established.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer sophism merely because an argument is false or unpersuasive.
Do not infer intention from the existence of a defect.
Do not infer concealment from a fallacious structure alone.
Do not create a canonical subtype taxonomy from the present source passage.
Do not silently repair the argument before evaluating it.
Do not treat reconstructed premises as direct quotations.
Do not infer that the defective conclusion is the author's own position.
Do not select the hidden or true teaching.
Do not collapse LC-010 into adjacent techniques.
operational.uncertainty_rule: When an argument appears defective but reconstruction, governing standard, material effect, attribution, or ordinary alternatives remain contestable, preserve a candidate sophistic structure. Intentionality remains unresolved.
operational.termination_rule: Terminate as NOT_TRIGGERED when no material argumentative defect is documented; as BLOCKED_MISSING_EVIDENCE when argument reconstruction, source, attribution, standard, counterfactual effect, or provenance is incomplete; as candidate when ordinary alternatives remain unresolved; and as corroborated only when the sophistic structure survives full documentary and adversarial testing. No runtime outcome in v0.1 establishes intentionality.

version_history.change_note: Initial executable projection of LC-010 with argument reconstruction, defect-standard and counterfactual testing, ordinary alternatives, corroboration, documentary subtype restraint, and an absolute separation between sophistic structure and inferred intentionality.
